RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion For A Court Order To Allow Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant'S Financial Condition In Support Of A Punitive Damage Claim.

Plaintiff John Doe's motion for a court order to allow pretrial discovery of defendant's financial condition is granted in part. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead. Although plaintiff did not explicitly request the relief recommended by Mr. Mead, that relief is explicitly provided for in the statute and can fairly be described as "lesser included" relief of the relief explicitly sought by plaintiff. The court discerns no legal prejudice to defendant Ramla Co., Ltd. by granting the relief recommended by Mr. Mead.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mari Bandoma Callado, Esq. and Emile A. Davis, Esq. (415-421-2800) of Dolan for Plaintiff.
Andrew W. Russel, Esq. (310-228-6049) of Fox Rotschild LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Ramla Co., Ltd.'s motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is DENIED. The court has specific jurisdiction over Ramla Co. based on the asserted misconduct committed by its chairman Akiro Murakawa and employee Makodo Takahashi in California that was directed toward plaintiff while he lived and worked in California. Plaintiff submitted evidence that Ramla Co. sent Mr. Murakawa, and its head chef, Mr. Kaga, to California to perform quality control by reviewing menus and decor at California restaurants including Ginto Izakaya Japonaise (Ginto) and that during that trip Mr. Murakawa forced plaintiff to drink beer despite plaintiff's explanation to Mr. Murakawa that his religious beliefs forbid him from drinking alcohol. (Plaintiff's Deposition, 24:7-21; Ueno Deposition, 32:10-13, 226:17-24; Ohmiya Declaration, 5.) Ramla Co. has not submitted persuasive evidence showing Mr. Murakawa was acting solely in his capacity as an investor of Ramla USA. The only evidence Ramla Co. cited in support of its argument is Mr. Ueno's deposition testimony that the incident took place at a "team-building event" at Ginto. (Ueno Deposition, 419:25-420:10.) Plaintiff also submitted evidence that Mr. Takahashi kicked plaintiff, forbade him from taking meal breaks, and threatened to kill him while working at Ginto in California. (Plaintiff's Deposition, 21:20-22:20, 22:21-23:17; Ohmiya Declaration, 12.) The foregoing evidence is sufficient to meet all the requirements for imposition of specific jurisdiction over Ramla Co. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)
